prompt_rules: world-map coastline tiles with curved land-water boundaries
include coast edges, corners, beaches, shallow water, small wave highlights, and broken shoreline variants
avoid straight vertical or horizontal strips of water next to land

prompt:
Bright mythical-Japan satoyama palette, soft warm GOLDEN-HOUR light, hand-painted Ghibli-like look. A top-down MOSSY STONE RETAINING WALL (苔むした石積み擁壁) autotile for terraced rice-paddy and shrine-approach settings. Every edge/corner frame carries the mandatory THREE-LAYER height stack: (A) COPING CAP / 笠石 — top 20-26px: neatly dressed flat pale-grey-green capstones running along the plateau edge, lush bright grass growing on the very top surface behind them, lit warmly from upper-left; each capstone has a subtle bevel highlight so the flat top is unmistakable; (B) VERTICAL STONE FACE — middle 60-76px: stacked rounded fieldstones (玉石 nozura-zumi) rendered with strong 3D volume — each cobble has a bright sunlit upper curve and a deep crevice shadow beneath, warm grey and grey-brown tones, soft green moss and tiny ferns in the joints; the wall face reads unmistakably as a forward-facing vertical surface with real depth, NOT a top-down rock texture; lower stones are darker and mossier; (C) BASE SHADOW — lower 18-24px: a warm dark contact shadow on the lower grass, anchoring the wall. Organic irregularity is essential: no two stones are the same size, the capstone band is slightly uneven, small wild plants sprout from cracks. CENTER tiles (Row1) = flat high-plateau surface matching tile.ka_grass hue — the top of the raised terrace, no wall visible. EDGE tiles (Row2, N/S/W/E) = the three-layer stack for each direction; the S-edge is the primary face tile. OUTER CORNER tiles (Row3, NW/NE/SW/SE) = ORGANICALLY CURVED outer corners — the capstone band curves around the corner in a gentle arc, fieldstones turn the corner naturally, the base shadow wraps around; ABSOLUTELY NO sharp 90-degree stone wall corners; the turn must look like a real mason curved the course. ACCENT tiles (Row4) = a mossy cracked section with a small fern, a section with a tiny stone-set drainage outlet, a capstone with a fallen flower, a lower-wall section with thick lichen. STRICT 4x4 layout, 16 tiles, each cell EXACTLY 128x128 px, filled edge to edge, FULLY OPAQUE, seamlessly tileable. No isometric angle, no vanishing point, no text, no characters.
Avoid: no Mario/Nintendo/Zelda/Final Fantasy/Pokemon/Dragon Quest/Secret of Mana/Okami/Sakuna characters, logos or likeness, no samurai armor, no kabuto helmet, no ninja outfit, no two-sword daisho, no gun, no modern objects, no horror mood, no dark gloomy palette, no bird or companion creature in the sheet, no letter emblems, no modern text/watermark/UI.
Output: crisp high-resolution 16-bit-style pixel art with fine readable pixel clusters, dark one-pixel outline, top-left light source, bright cheerful colors, solid #FF00FF magenta background filling all empty space, consistent identity and scale across frames.
